title: Rotate and Remove Duplicate Rotated Subarrays

statement.markdown: Given an array of integers, rotate it to the right by one position, and then remove any duplicate rotated subarrays. A rotated subarray is a contiguous subarray of equal elements which is a rotation of some other subarray. Return the length of the resulting array.
statement.outputFormat: Output the length of the resulting array
statement.notes: Input array size up to 10000, non-zero elements

hints.text: Use the property of the array rotation and duplicates to reduce the size of the array.

editorial.title: Editorial Title
editorial.intuition: We can solve this problem in O(n) time. First, we find the first occurrence of the duplicate subarray.
editorial.bruteForce: Time complexity O(n*n*n), not acceptable for this problem.
editorial.betterSolution: We can solve this problem using a sliding window approach.
editorial.optimalSolution: Optimal solution description: We can maintain a set to track the current window of unique elements and shrink the window whenever we encounter a duplicate.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: Correctness proof: We can prove our solution correct by considering all possible scenarios.
editorial.dryRun: Example run of our solution: given array [3, 6, 2, 7, 7, 3], our solution returns [7, 7, 3, 3, 6, 2].
